channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Inflation expected to climb to highest level in 2 years
item.description: The federal government delivers a cost-of-living report Friday. A spike in gasoline prices triggered by the war with Iran is expected to push inflation to its highest level in nearly two years.

item.title: Iran war live: Israel attacks Lebanon again on eve of US-Iran truce talks
item.description: Iran’s parliament speaker warns that ‘time is running out’ as Israel continues to bombard Lebanon.

item.title: Torpedo-like object discovered by Indonesian fisherman
item.description: A fisherman in Indonesia discovered a torpedo-like object near the Lombok Strait, a waterway between Bali and Lombok.

item.title: Six months into ‘ceasefire’, Gaza suffers under persistent Israeli attacks
item.description: Gaza's devastation grows as over 72,000 people killed and 172,000 injured amid Israeli violations of US-brokered truce.

item.title: Senegal PM labels Trump ‘agent of global destabilisation’
item.description: Senegalese prime minister Ousmane Sonko accused Donald Trump of plunging the world into “chaos”.

item.title: Defense executives worry Trump’s proposed military splurge could backfire
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: Gen Z watched millennials burn out at their desk—now 1 in 4 are ditching office jobs for trade jobs
item.description: Gen Z was sold the dream of white-collar work. Now 75% associate it with instability—they're ditching desk jobs for drains and electrical cables.

item.title: U.S. and Iran begin peace talks as Trump’s White House goes to war against the media, insider traders, and the Pope
item.description: Everything you need to know before you reach the office this morning.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Shows Off Friends, Weapons Before Trump’s China Trip
item.description: Kim Jong Un is showcasing his tighter ties with old allies and battlefield lessons from Ukraine, as he positions North Korea as a strong nuclear power before Donald Trump’s trip to China.

item.title: Oil to Test Wartime Highs If Hormuz Standstill Drags, JPM Warns
item.description: Oil looks set to test levels seen at the height of the war in the Middle East if cargoes shipped through the Strait of Hormuz take until July to fully recover, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Taiwan Exports, US Surplus Hit Records as AI Outweighs War
item.description: Taiwan’s exports jumped to an all-time high as rampant global demand for artificial intelligence chips eclipsed supply chain uncertainties unleashed by the US war against Iran.

item.title: TSMC Sales Beat Estimates After War Fails to Dent AI Demand
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 35% increase in quarterly revenue, suggesting global AI chip demand remained intact during the first weeks of war in the Middle East.

item.title: China Revamps ChiNext Rules to Lure More Startups to Market
item.description: China unveiled new rules for the Shenzhen stock exchange’s tech-focused ChiNext board, seeking to broaden listing options and make the market more attractive to fast‑growing companies.

item.title: UBS Wins Dismissal of Credit Suisse Money-Laundering Charges Tied to Mozambique Tuna Bond Scandal
item.description: UBS Group AG won dismissal of a money-laundering case it inherited from its troubled former rival Credit Suisse linked to the so-called Mozambique tuna-bond scandal.

item.title: Japan Lowers View of China Relations Amid Tension Over Taiwan
item.description: Japan slightly downgraded its assessment of relations with China in its annual foreign policy report released Friday, as ties between the two Asian giants remain strained over views on Taiwan.

item.title: Rubio to Visit India in May as Ties With New Delhi Improve
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit India in May as New Delhi and Washington seek to reset ties strained by US tariff policies.

item.title: Investors Emboldened as Iran Ceasefire Appears to Hold
item.description: US stocks have climbed for seven straight days.

item.title: Gold May See Long-Term Rebound Despite War Setbacks, Banks Say
item.description: Gold is likely to rebound in the long-term even as the Middle East war upended the market, according to banks including ANZ Banking Group Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain After Iran Attacks Lower Saudi Output Capacity
item.description: Oil rose for a second day after Saudi Arabia said a recent wave of Iranian attacks slashed its production capacity, tempering this week’s sharp plunge triggered by a US-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: Saudi Says Attacks on Oil Assets Hit Output and Exports
item.description: Saudi Arabia said a series of attacks on its critical energy infrastructure disrupted oil and gas production and hit supply to global markets that are already reeling from the Iran war.

item.title: 冲绳县要求早日归还普天间机场 日美协议将满30年
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】日美两国政府就美军普天间机场（冲绳县宜野湾市）全面归还达成协议到12日将满30年，冲绳县知事玉城丹尼在10日的记者会上要求早日实现归还。玉城表示：“县民至今仍承受着沉重负担。绝不允许普天间机场被固定下来。”

item.title: 日本天皇依惯例在皇宫苗圃播撒稻种
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】日本天皇9日在皇宫内生物学研究所旁边苗圃里，播下了粳米“日本胜”和糯米“满月糯”的稻种。这是旨在鼓励农耕的例行活动。据宫内厅称，稻种将在出苗后的5月前后插秧，预计9月进行收割。　　天皇身

item.title: 三名日本导演携新作角逐戛纳电影节“金棕榈奖”
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】将于5月举行的第79届法国戛纳国际电影节中，日本导演是枝裕和的《盒子里的羊》（SHEEP IN THE BOX）、滨口龙介的《突如其来》（ALL OF SUDDEN）、深田晃司的《奈义日记》（NAGI NOTES）入选主竞赛单元，将与

item.title: Росія ігнорує запити Польщі щодо уламків літака, що розбився під Смоленськом – посол
item.description: Посол Польщі у Росії Кшиштоф Краєвський заявив, що росіяни ігнорують прохання Польщі про повернення уламків президентського літака.

item.title: Рубіо подякував Румунії та Болгарії за підтримку на Близькому Сході
item.description: Державний секретар США Марко Рубіо провів розмови зі своєю колегою з Румунії Оаною Цою та виконувачем обов'язків прем'єр-міністра Болгарії Андреєм Гюровим щодо американських операцій на Близькому Сході.

item.title: Поліція Мюнхена розслідує антисемітський мотив у нападі на ізраїльський ресторан
item.description: У Мюнхені невідомі кинули піротехніку у будівлю ізраїльського ресторану, поліція підозрює, що мотивом нападу був антисемітизм.

item.title: ЗМІ: ЄС та США готують угоду щодо рідкісноземельних мінералів
item.description: Європейський Союз і США наближаються до укладення угоди про координацію дій щодо видобутку та забезпечення поставок рідкісноземельних мінералів.

item.title: У Франції триває суперечка про проєкт щодо дозволу працювати на 1 травня
item.description: У Національних зборах Франції продовжується боротьба за проєкт про те, щоб дозволити деяким бізнесам відкриватися 1 травня, яке в країні традиційно є майже тотальним вихідним днем.

item.title: Німеччина призупинила дію скандальної норми про виїзд чоловіків за кордон
item.description: Міністерство оборони Німеччини тимчасово скасувало вимогу щодо обов’язкового отримання дозволу від Бундесверу для тривалого виїзду за кордон для чоловіків 17-45 років.

item.title: Зеленський вважає, що Європі для безпеки потрібні Україна, Британія, Норвегія і Туреччина
item.description: Президент України Володимир Зеленський висловив думку, що у разі подальшого розпаду усталеної архітектури безпеки країн Заходу Європі для реальної безпеки потрібне буде повернення Британії, а також Україна, Норвегія і Туреччина.

item.title: Естонія заявила про успішну кампанію України проти російської ППО
item.description: Глава Департаменту оборонної готовності Естонії Герт Каю оцінив досягнення українських військових у війні проти Росії у квітні.

item.title: ‘An abomination’: the Lancashire town kicking up at stink from reopened landfill
item.description:
Residents of Fleetwood say continuous foul smell from Transwaste site is causing illness and making life hell

In the week that many families went to the coast for the fresh sea air or the tang of fish and chips, visitors to one Lancashire resort inhaled a rather more unpleasant aroma.

“Welcome to Fleetwood,” read the local newspaper headline. “The town that smells of bin juice.”

Continue reading...

item.title: Domestic abuser jailed for killing wife who took her own life in Scotland
item.description:
Lee Milne sentenced to eight years in landmark case after being found guilty of culpable homicide of Kimberly Milne

A man convicted of killing his wife, who took her own life after repeated domestic abuse, has been jailed for eight years in case seen as a significant legal milestone.

Kimberly Milne, 28, died when she jumped from a bridge in July 2023. Her estranged husband, Lee Milne, was found guilty of culpable homicide last month after a trial at the high court in Glasgow.

In the UK, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 116 123 and the domestic abuse helpline is 0808 2000 247. In the US, the suicide prevention lifeline is 988 and the domestic violence hotline is 1-800-799-SAFE (7233). In Australia, the crisis support service Lifeline is on 13 11 14 and the national family violence counselling service is on 1800 737 732. Other international helplines can be found via www.befrienders.org

Continue reading...

item.title: Dolce & Gabbana says co-founder Stefano Gabbana quit as chair at start of year
item.description:
Designer said to be considering options for his 40% stake in fashion house ahead of negotiations with bank lenders

Stefano Gabbana left his post as chair of Dolce & Gabbana at the start of this year, the design house he co-founded with his then partner, Domenico Dolce, in 1985 has said.

The Italian luxury fashion house said Gabbana had tendered his resignation, effective as of 1 January, “as part of a natural evolution of its organisational structure and governance”.

Continue reading...

item.title: Victim of ‘disgusting paedophile’ serving Australia’s longest sentence for child sex abuse tells of ‘lost decades’
item.description:
Woman tells court ‘I lost my teenage self’ from abuse by teacher William ‘Rob’ Gilfillan, already in prison for offences against daughter

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

A woman who was repeatedly sexually assaulted in the 1980s by her high school PE teacher has told a court how the man’s actions resulted in her life being changed “negatively for decades”.

In December, William “Rob” Gilfillan was found guilty of indecent assault of a person under 16 and sexual penetration of a child under 16. The five counts against two victims took place at Traralgon high school in Gippsland.

Continue reading...

item.title: Xi tells Taiwan opposition leader people on both sides of strait are Chinese in rare meeting
item.description:
Cheng Li-wun’s visit to Beijing has sparked controversy in Taiwan, with critics accusing her of being too close to China

In a rare meeting with Taiwan’s opposition leader, China’s president, Xi Jinping, declared that people on both sides of the Taiwan Strait were Chinese and wanted peace.

The meeting in Beijing between Xi and Cheng Li-wun, the chair of Taiwan’s Kuomintang (KMT), is the first such contact in a decade. The visit has sparked controversy in Taiwan, with Cheng’s critics accusing her of being too close to China, a country that many in Taiwan see as a threat.

Continue reading...

item.title: Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds
item.description:
Powerful storm brings destruction, while temperatures soar in Vietnam and torrential rain lashes South Korea

Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila, currently in the Solomon Sea, is expected to continue moving south-westwards over the coming days. According to the Australian Bureau of Meteorology, Maila had peak sustained winds of 115mph (185km/h), with gusts up to 160mph on Thursday, making it the strongest cyclone recorded this far north in the Solomon Sea.

The storm has caused widespread damage across the Solomon Islands, particularly in Western, Choiseul and Isabel provinces, where schools, clinics and homes have been damaged. The government is prioritising humanitarian assistance after about 120 people were displaced and almost 73,000 people affected overall.

Continue reading...
